LZF5-NPP: Francis Settle I was born in 1622 in Barwick in Elmet, Yorkshire, England. Sometime before 1657 he crossed the Atlantic, leaving England behind to begin a new life in Virginia. He died in 1707 in Richmond, Virginia, having lived to the age of eighty-five.
LRB5-HFB: Isaac Chase was born in 1650 in Hampton, New Hampshire, and made his living as a blacksmith, working at the forge in Vineyard Haven on Martha's Vineyard. A Quaker by faith, he settled on the island and died there in Tisbury in 1727.
L2C7-PST: Col Thomas Gibson was born in 1667 in Isle of Wight, Virginia. He was an active member of his colonial community, appearing repeatedly in the records of St. Peter's Parish in New Kent County during the 1690s, where he was paid by the parish and vestry and listed among the tithables tasked with clearing the road. In 1711 he had land processioned in St. Paul's Parish, Hanover County, where he died in 1734.
L614-XYM: John Francis Settle Sr. was born in 1659 in Richmond, Virginia, and made his living as a tobacco planter. He spent his life in colonial Virginia and died in 1738 in King George County.
L5XJ-6JS: John Sayles was born around 1633, likely in England, and settled in colonial Rhode Island, where he served as town treasurer from 1659 to 1660. He married a daughter of Roger Williams, the founder of Providence, a connection documented in a 1670 deed in which he noted that his father-in-law had given him forest land. He and his wife raised ten children before his death in 1681 in Newport at the age of forty-eight.
MK15-ZKQ: Mary Butler was born in 1635 in Stratford, Connecticut Colony. She lived in colonial New England and New York, and died in 1676 in Hempstead Town, Queens, New York Colony.
GQQ4-MY8: Grace Heaton was born in 1667 in Yorkshire, England, where she made her home before crossing to America. She settled in colonial Pennsylvania and died in 1708 in Langhorne, Middletown Township, Bucks County, where she was laid to rest in the Middletown Friends Cemetery.
LCRS-BDW: Johannes Christian Lehman was born in 1700 in Speyerdorf in the Rhineland-Palatinate of Germany, and was baptized that June in nearby Lachen. He was a twin, born alongside his brother Christian K. In 1730 and 1731 he emigrated to America, arriving in Philadelphia, where he settled and ultimately died in 1748.
LCM2-P3L: Philadelphia Lea was born in 1682 in Essex County, Virginia. She lived her entire life in that colonial Virginia community and died there in 1754.
LZ6B-N3B: John Hodge I was born in 1643 in Windsor, Connecticut Colony. As a young child he arrived in Boston in 1650, growing up in colonial Massachusetts. He died in 1694 in Newbury, Essex County.
GHCN-W3F: Mary Frances Allen was born in 1662 in Doswell, Hanover County, Virginia. In 1695 she appeared in the records of St. Peter's Parish in New Kent County, where she was paid by the parish. She lived her whole life in the Doswell area and died there in 1734.
LZ2X-W2V: Edith Squire was born in 1587 in Charlton Mackrell, Somerset, England. In 1638 she emigrated to America aboard the ship Mary & John together with her husband Henry Adams and their large family of sons and a daughter. Through that marriage she became a great-grandmother of the patriot Samuel Adams. She died in 1673 in Medfield, Massachusetts Bay Colony.
L6NV-5LH: Thomas Broughton was born in 1614 in Longdon, Staffordshire, England. He eventually crossed to colonial New England and died in 1700 in Boston, Massachusetts Bay Colony.
LB96-7LB: Marguerite Marie Martin was born in 1622 in Limbourg, in the Liège region of Belgium, and lived for a time in Bastogne. She later crossed the Atlantic and died in 1681 in Piscataway Township, Middlesex County, New Jersey.
KZ28-LRY: Susanna Newhall was born in 1624 in Sherington, Buckinghamshire, England, where she was baptized that April. She crossed to colonial New England and lived in Lynn, Essex County, Massachusetts, where she died in 1682.
9VH7-H1T: William Brereton, 2nd Lord Brereton of Leighlin, was born in 1611 in Brereton cum Smethwick, Cheshire, England. He held the noble titles of Baron Brereton and 2nd Lord Brereton. He died in 1664 in his ancestral Cheshire home, with his estate going to probate that same year.
LRYW-G84: Antoine de Saussure Perronette de Crocketagne was born in 1643 in Montauban-de-Luchon in the Haute-Garonne region of France. He later left France and died in 1735 in County Londonderry, in the north of Ireland.
KNCG-7S1: Richard Austin II was born in 1598 in Titchfield, Hampshire, England, where he worked as a tailor. In May 1636 he emigrated from Southampton aboard the ship Bevis of Hampton, traveling with his wife Elizabeth, their two young sons, and his sister and her children. He settled in Massachusetts Bay Colony and died in Charlestown in 1638.
LBLQ-PMB: Gregory Baxter was born in 1606 in Staffordshire, England, and crossed to colonial Massachusetts. In Roxbury he was admitted a Freeman, taking his oath on March 6, 1631. He died in 1659 in Braintree, Massachusetts Bay Colony.
LY2Q-LMM: Edmund Bridges was born in 1611 in London, England. In 1635 he emigrated to America aboard the ship James, arriving at Lynn in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He settled in colonial Massachusetts and died in 1684 in Ipswich, Essex County, where his will was recorded the following year.
99D7-YVR: Jane Tapp was born in 1627 in Benington, Hertfordshire, England. She crossed to colonial New England and in April 1649 was admitted to the First Church of Christ of Milford. She lived in Milford, New Haven Colony, where she died in 1703.
LX7G-VPS: Sir Hugh Owen, 2nd Baronet, was born in 1645 in Orielton, Pembrokeshire, Wales, and held the title of Baronet. He died in 1698 in Bristol, England.
L5LT-1HB: Sir William Underwood was born in 1606 in London, England, and became a merchant, planter, and colonial leader. He served as a Member of the House of Burgesses in 1632 and emigrated in 1642. He died in 1639 in Essex, England.
KN3V-B53: Christopher Webb II was born in 1630 in Crediton, Devon, England, and worked as a millwright. He crossed to colonial New England, where he served as an Ensign in the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He died in 1694 in Braintree, Suffolk County.
LCJ4-YHP: Thomas Stevenson I was born in 1615 in London, England. He came to America and joined the colony, led in part by Capt. Underhill, that settled Southold on Long Island. Having secured a homestead, he married the widow Mary Bernard at the Reformed Dutch Church in New Amsterdam on August 15, 1645. He died in 1668 in Newtown, Queens, New York Colony.
LZFW-NQB: Thomas Chase was born in 1616 in Chesham, Buckinghamshire, England. He crossed to colonial New England and died young, in 1652, in Hampton, Massachusetts Bay Colony.
LTZJ-XY7: Sgt John Woods was born in 1610 in Sudbury, Suffolk, England, and in 1635 arrived at Salem aboard the ship Hopewell. He removed to Sudbury, Massachusetts, in 1638 and was admitted a Freeman there in 1642, later relocating to Marlborough in 1660. He drew up his will in November 1677 and died the following year in 1678 in Marlborough, Middlesex County.
KVGX-5D1: Susanna Cooke was born in 1616 in St Giles without Cripplegate, Middlesex, England, where she was baptized that July. She crossed to colonial New England and died in 1682 in Haverhill, Essex County, Massachusetts.
